title: Keiko's Big Feelings Day
synopsis: When Keiko's favorite stuffed bunny goes missing, she experiences big feelings of worry and sadness. With help from her diverse family members, she learns that it's okay to feel scared and that asking for help makes her brave.

pages.text: Keiko loved her soft white bunny. Every morning, Bunny sat on her bed, ready for hugs. But today, Bunny was gone! Keiko looked under her pillow. No Bunny.

illustration.description: A young Japanese girl with short black hair in pink pajamas sits on her unmade bed, looking puzzled. Her bedroom has soft yellow walls with colorful drawings taped up. The bed has rainbow sheets and a small indent where a stuffed animal usually sits. Bright morning sunlight streams through the window.
illustration.alt: Keiko in pajamas looking confused on her colorful bedroom bed

pages.text: Keiko felt worried. Her tummy felt funny, like butterflies were dancing inside. 'Mama!' she called. 'I can't find Bunny!' Keiko's eyes filled with tears.

illustration.description: Close-up of Keiko's expressive face showing worry, with wide dark eyes and a slight frown. Her hands are pressed to her tummy. Behind her, toys are scattered around the bright room. The illustration uses soft blues and grays to show her worried mood against the cheerful yellow background.
illustration.alt: Keiko looking worried with hands on her tummy

pages.text: Mama came running. She had kind brown eyes and gentle hands. 'Oh sweetie, you look so sad,' Mama said, giving Keiko a warm hug. 'Tell me what's wrong.'

illustration.description: A loving scene of Keiko being hugged by her mother, a woman with warm brown skin and curly black hair pulled back in a ponytail. She wears a soft green sweater. They're sitting on the rainbow-colored bed, surrounded by morning sunlight. The mood is comforting with warm yellows and soft greens.
illustration.alt: Keiko being comforted by her caring mother on the bed

pages.text: 'Bunny is lost!' Keiko cried. 'I'm scared she's gone forever!' Papa appeared in the doorway. He had a friendly beard and wore a blue shirt. 'Let's be brave detectives and find Bunny together,' he said with a smile.

illustration.description: Keiko crying while Mama holds her, with Papa (a tall man with olive skin, dark beard, and kind eyes) standing in the doorway. He's wearing a bright blue button-up shirt and has his sleeves rolled up. The room shows the family coming together, with warm oranges and blues creating a supportive atmosphere.
illustration.alt: Family gathering as Papa offers to help find the missing bunny

pages.text: The family searched everywhere. They looked in the toy box, behind the couch, even in the kitchen. Big sister Yuki, who had long braids, checked the laundry basket. 'Found her!' Yuki shouted happily.

illustration.description: The family searching together throughout a cozy living room with diverse furniture and colorful decorations. Yuki, a teenager with long black braids and a purple t-shirt, holds up a white stuffed bunny triumphantly from a wicker laundry basket. Everyone's faces show relief and joy. Bright, celebratory colors fill the scene.
illustration.alt: Yuki holding up the found bunny while the family celebrates

pages.text: Keiko felt so happy! She hugged Bunny tight and thanked her family. 'I was scared,' she said, 'but asking for help made me brave.' Mama smiled. 'Big feelings are okay, and we're always here to help.'

illustration.description: A heartwarming group hug with Keiko in the center clutching her white bunny, surrounded by her loving family. All faces show pure joy and relief. The scene is bathed in golden afternoon light streaming through windows, with warm yellows, soft pinks, and gentle greens creating a mood of love and security.
illustration.alt: Happy family group hug with Keiko holding her recovered bunny

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did Keiko feel when Bunny was missing?
questions.explanation: Keiko felt worried and sad when she couldn't find her favorite bunny. Her tummy felt funny and she had tears in her eyes.

questions.question: Who found Bunny?
questions.explanation: Yuki, Keiko's big sister with long braids, found Bunny in the laundry basket and shouted 'Found her!' happily.

questions.question: What did Papa suggest they become to find Bunny?
questions.explanation: Papa suggested they become 'brave detectives' to find Bunny together, which helped make the search feel like a fun adventure.

questions.question: What did Keiko learn about asking for help?
questions.explanation: Keiko learned that asking for help when you're scared actually makes you brave. Her family was there to support her through her big feelings.

pages.title: Fill in the Feelings
pages.instructions: Help complete the story by choosing the right feeling words!
pages.text: Keiko felt ____ when Bunny was missing. But when her family helped her search, she felt ____. Finding Bunny made everyone feel ____!

pages.title: Who Helped Keiko?
pages.instructions: Remember who was in Keiko's family and helped find Bunny!
pages.text: ____ gave Keiko a warm hug. ____ suggested being brave detectives. ____ found Bunny in the laundry basket!
